mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities by lowering discount rates and supporting valuation expansion, so declines in FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS declines versus the prior month, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above the recent inflation trend, real policy rates rise relative to nominal rates and equity multiples compress, so inflation surprises predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS minus 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if adding CPIAUCSL above FEDFUNDS does not improve hit rate versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in unemployment signals weakening household income and tighter earnings expectations, which depresses near-term risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if SPX has positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of UNRATE-rise triggers, or if the signal loses all predictive power in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A broad deterioration in retail sales alongside housing starts weakens cyclical demand expectations and lowers forward earnings revisions, so simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both fall versus their 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal does not outperform either series alone and triggered cases do not show a statistically negative 20-day return skew.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving real activity and corporate revenue momentum, supporting cyclical earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO over the prior month is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth turns positive after a weak stretch, investors revise recession odds lower and bid equities higher, so quarterly GDP acceleration predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive on a q/q annualized basis after a prior negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP reacceleration episodes do not precede above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal vanishes outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth together with a low unemployment rate signals resilient labor income and supports earnings confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE is at or below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-unemployment condition fails to beat random baseline on 20-day returns or if positive returns do not dominate when both labor indicators are supportive.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive policy regime, inflation and activity surprises both matter: when CPIAUCSL stays elevated while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, equity multiples compress and short-horizon S&P 500 returns turn negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if, under restrictive policy, the triggered cases do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if including INDPRO does not improve signal purity.
